revision.text:
[[de:Chentet-cheret]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W17:t*t W12 D21:t N14-N33:N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Chentet-cheret
''Ḫnt.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Ship'' }} '''Chentet-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Ship (constellation)|de|Schiff (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|qid=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}. It was located approximately at the same height as the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} in this decan is the {{ill|Binary star|en|Binary star|qid=Q50053|lt=Binary star|1=Binary star}} system {{ill|Zeta Scorpii|de|ζ2-Scorpii|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zeta Scorpii|qq=}} in the constellation {{ill|Scorpius|en|Scorpius|qid=Q8865|lt=Scorpius|1=Scorpius}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Chentet-cheret represented the 11th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=16th Shemu IV|de|16. Schemu IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 89. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Chentet-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:チェンテト・チェレト]] [[de:Chentet-cheret]] {{wikidata link|Q1070112}}
